Euphorbia nigrihorrida is a species of succulent plant in the Euphorbiaceae family, characterized by its dark green, warty stems and small, white flowers. The plant has been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, with potential health benefits including wound healing and protection against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Euphorbia nigrihorrida include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which may contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Euphorbia nigrihorrida has been used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and salves, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
